title: Cat Panting with Open Mouth at Rest: Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: If your cat is panting with an open mouth at rest, it is a high-level veterinary emergency. Learn why and how to seek immediate help.
content_html:
TL;DR: Open-mouth breathing in cats is a life-threatening emergency that indicates severe respiratory distress or pain. If your cat is panting, seek immediate veterinary care at the nearest emergency animal hospital.

What does it mean if my cat is open-mouth panting?

Open-mouth breathing or panting in cats is a clinical sign where a cat breathes through their mouth rather than their nose. Unlike dogs, who pant to regulate body temperature, cats only pant when they are under extreme physiological stress, experiencing severe pain, or suffering from significant respiratory distress.

Is open-mouth breathing in cats a medical emergency?

  • Urgency Level: High. If your cat is panting while at rest, this is a life-threatening emergency.
  • Cats often hide illness until their oxygen levels are dangerously low and they are in significant distress.
  • Common underlying causes include congestive heart failure, feline asthma, pneumonia, or physical trauma.
  • You should seek immediate veterinary care at the nearest emergency animal hospital.

Why should I take a video of my cat’s breathing for the triage team?

  • If it is safe to do so without delaying your departure, capture a 10-second video of your cat’s breathing.
  • A video allows the veterinarian to assess the rhythm, the involvement of abdominal muscles, and the cat's posture.
  • Sharing this video helps the triage team determine the severity of the respiratory effort and prepare life-saving equipment before you arrive.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

In cats, labored, open-mouth breathing at rest indicates a significant loss of pulmonary function and impending pulmonary arrest, warranting immediate emergency triage. This is considered a life-threatening respiratory emergency. Such patients should be moved to the treatment area, regardless of other physical findings.

Chapter: Emergency, Respiratory

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1663)

faqs.question: Can cats pant just because they are hot?
faqs.answer: While cats can pant due to extreme heat or stress, it is much rarer than in dogs and should still be treated as an emergency if it occurs while the cat is resting or doesn't resolve instantly in a cool environment.

faqs.question: What should I do during the car ride to the vet?
faqs.answer: Keep the car cool and quiet. Do not attempt to restrain the cat or cover their face, as this can increase stress and further restrict their breathing.

faqs.question: What are the signs of 'air hunger' in cats?
faqs.answer: Signs include an extended neck, elbows pointed outward, open-mouth breathing, and blue or pale-tinged gums.
